data.title: Torrid GAAP EPS of -$0.01 misses by $0.05, revenue of $263.8M misses by $20.49M
data.description:
• Torrid press release (NYSE:CURV): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.01 misses by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $263.8M (-12.4% Y/Y) misses by $20.49M.

  • For the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024 the Company expects:

  • Net sales between $255.0 million and $270.0 million.

  • Adjusted EBITDA between $9.0 million and $15.0 million.

  • For the full year 2024, which has 52 weeks compared to 53 weeks in full year 2023, the Company expects:

  • Net sales between $1.083 billion and $1.098 billion.
  • Adjusted EBITDA between $101.0 million and $107.0 million.
  • Capital expenditures between $20 million and $25 million reflecting infrastructure and technology investments as well as new stores for the year.

data.title: Okta surges as Q3 results, guidance blow past estimates
data.description:
Okta (NASDAQ:OKTA) rose 17.5% in late trading on Tuesday after the cybersecurity company reported fiscal third-quarter results and guidance that topped expectations and upped its full-year outlook.

For fiscal 2025, Okta now expects to earn between $2.75 and $2.76 per share on an adjusted basis, up from a prior range of $2.58 to $2.63 per share. Full-year revenue is now forecast to be between $2.595B up $2.597B, up 15% year-over-year. The company previously said it expected sales to be between $2.56B and $2.57B.

Analysts were expecting $2.61 per share and $2.56B in revenue.

Looking to the fourth-quarter, Okta expects to earn between $0.73 and $0.74 on an adjusted basis, with revenue between $667M and $669M. Analysts were anticipating adjusted earnings of $0.67 per share and $650.7M.

The stronger-than-anticipated guidance comes on the back of a better-than-expected third-quarter for Okta, which has suffered from some data breaches in recent memory.

For the period ending October 31, Okta said it earned an adjusted $0.67 per share as revenue rose 14% year-over-year to come in at $665M. Subscriptions accounted for $651M in revenue during the period, while professional services and other revenue was $14M.

Analysts were expecting earnings of $0.59 per share on $635.2M in revenue.

“The focused investments we’ve made in our partner ecosystem, the public sector vertical, and large customers are materializing in our business with each of these areas contributing meaningfully to top-line growth,” Okta Chief Executive Officer and co-founder Todd McKinnon said in a statement. “Okta’s commitment to innovation and elevating identity security is resonating with customers of all sizes as they look to Okta to modernize their identity infrastructure.”

The company will hold a conference call at 5 p.m. EST to discuss the results.

data.title: Box Non-GAAP EPS of $0.45 beats by $0.03, revenue of $275.9M beats by $0.8M
data.description:
• Box press release (NYSE:BOX): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.45 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $275.9M (+5.3% Y/Y) beats by $0.8M.
  • Shares -4.68%.

  • Remaining Performance Obligations of $1.3 Billion, up 13% Year-Over-Year, up 14% in Constant Currency

    Record GAAP Operating Margin of 8.5% and Record Non-GAAP Operating Margin of 29.1%

    GAAP Net Income Per Share of $0.05

  • Q4 FY25 Guidance

    • Revenue is expected to be approximately $279 million, up 6% year-over-year, or 7% growth on a constant currency basis.
    • GAAP operating margin is expected to be approximately 7.5%, and non-GAAP operating margin is expected to be approximately 27.5%.
    • GAAP net income per share attributable to common stockholders is expected to be approximately $0.07. GAAP EPS guidance includes an expected negative impact of $0.02 from unfavorable exchange rates and $0.01 from the recognition of deferred tax expenses in international countries.
    • Non-GAAP diluted net income per share attributable to common stockholders is expected to be approximately $0.41. Non-GAAP EPS guidance includes an expected negative impact of $0.02 from unfavorable exchange rates and $0.01 from the recognition of deferred tax expenses in international countries.
    • Weighted-average diluted shares outstanding are expected to be approximately 151 million.

    Full Year FY25 Guidance

    • Revenue is expected to be approximately $1.090 billion, up 5% year-over-year, or 7% growth on a constant currency basis. We now expect FX to be a 190 basis point headwind to full fiscal year 2025 revenue growth, 20 basis points higher than our previous expectations. On a constant currency basis, our new guidance represents a $3 million increase from our previous guidance.
    • GAAP operating margin is expected to be approximately 7.5%, and non-GAAP operating margin is expected to be approximately 28%. For full fiscal year 2025 GAAP and non-GAAP operating margin, we now expect FX to be a headwind of 140 basis points, 10 basis points higher than our previous expectations.
    • GAAP net income per share attributable to common stockholders is expected to be approximately $0.30. FY25 GAAP EPS guidance includes an expected negative impact of $0.13 from unfavorable exchange rates and $0.05 from the recognition of deferred tax expenses in international countries.
    • Non-GAAP diluted net income per share attributable to common stockholders is expected to be approximately $1.70. FY25 non-GAAP EPS guidance includes an expected negative impact of $0.13 from unfavorable exchange rates and $0.05 from the recognition of deferred tax expenses in international countries.
    • Weighted-average diluted shares outstanding are expected to be approximately 149 million.
    • Q4 EPS consensus is $0.41
    • Q4 revenue consensus is $278.60M
    • FY24 EPS consensus is $1.67
    • FY24 revenue consensus is $1.09B

data.title: Salesforce Non-GAAP EPS of $2.41 misses by $0.04, revenue of $9.44B beats by $90M
data.description:
• Salesforce press release (NYSE:CRM): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.41 misses by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $9.44B (+8.3% Y/Y) beats by $90M.
  • Shares -2.08%.
  • Third Quarter GAAP Operating Margin of 20.0% and non-GAAP Operating Margin of 33.1%
  • Current Remaining Performance Obligation of $26.4 Billion, up 10% both Y/Y & in CC
  • Third Quarter Operating Cash Flow of $1.98 Billion, up 29% Y/Y, and Free Cash Flow of $1.78 Billion, up 30% Y/Y
  • Returned $1.2 Billion in the Form of Share Repurchases and $0.4 Billion in Dividend Payments to Stockholders, Third Quarter Total Cash Returned to Stockholders of $1.6 Billion

    • FY25 Guidance Highlights

    • Initiates Fourth Quarter FY25 Revenue Guidance of $9.90 Billion - $10.10 Billion, up 7% - 9% Y/Y. Q4 revenue consensus is $10.05B
    • Raises Low End of Full Year FY25 Revenue Guidance to $37.8 Billion to $38.0 Billion, up 8% - 9% Y/Y and Maintains Full Year FY25 Subscription & Support Revenue Growth Guidance of Slightly Below 10% Y/Y & Approximately 10% in CC. FY24 revenue consensus is $37.86B
    • Raises Full Year FY25 GAAP Operating Margin Guidance to 19.8% and Raises non-GAAP Operating Margin Guidance to 32.9%
    • Raises Full Year FY25 Operating Cash Flow Growth Guidance to 24% to 26% Y/Y
      • Non-GAAP Diluted Net Income per Share: Q4 $2.57 - $2.62, Q4 EPS consensus is $2.65
      • FY guidance $9.98 - $10.03, FY24 EPS consensus is $10.11

data.title: Okta Non-GAAP EPS of $0.67 beats by $0.09, revenue of $665M beats by $15.36M
data.description:
• Okta press release (NASDAQ:OKTA): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.67 beats by $0.09.
  • Revenue of $665M (+13.9% Y/Y) beats by $15.36M.

  • For the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025, the Company expects:

  • Total revenue of $667 million to $669 million, representing a growth rate of 10% to 11% year-over-year;

  • Current RPO of $2.130 billion to $2.135 billion, representing a growth rate of 9% year-over-year;

  • Non-GAAP operating income of $154 million to $156 million, yielding a non-GAAP operating margin of 23%;

  • Non-GAAP diluted net income per share of $0.73 to $0.74, assuming diluted weighted-average shares outstanding of approximately 182 million and a non-GAAP tax rate of 26%; and

  • Non-GAAP free cash flow margin of approximately 32%.

  • For the full year fiscal 2025, the Company now expects:

  • Total revenue of $2.595 billion to $2.597 billion, representing a growth rate of 15% year-over-year;
  • Non-GAAP operating income of $573 million to $575 million, yielding a non-GAAP operating margin of 22%;
  • Non-GAAP diluted net income per share of $2.75 to $2.76, assuming diluted weighted-average shares outstanding of approximately 182 million and a non-GAAP tax rate of 26%; and
  • Non-GAAP free cash flow margin of approximately 25%.

data.title: Roivant unit to cease development of namilumab for sarcoidosis
data.description:
• Roivant’s (NASDAQ:ROIV) Kinevant Sciences subsidiary said a Phase 2 study of its drug candidate namilumab failed to meet its primary endpoint.
  • The study failed to show a treatment benefit for namilumab in patients with chronic active pulmonary sarcoidosis, according to a statement.
  • As a result, Kinevant plans to discontinue development of namilumab for the treatment of sarcoidosis.

data.title: SEC committee to examine arbitration clauses, alternative assets
data.description:
The Securities and Exchange Commission's Investor Advisory Committee scheduled a Dec. 10 meeting that will host two panels, the SEC said Tuesday.

One will examine the use of mandatory arbitration clauses by registered investment advisers.

The other will discuss the mainstreaming of alternative assets to retail investors, a statement from the SEC said.

Established by the Dodd-Frank Act after the 2008 financial crisis, the Investor Advisory Committee, which focuses on investor-related interests, advises the SEC on regulatory priorities and initiatives to help protect investors and promote the integrity of the U.S. securities markets.

The meeting, which will start at 10 AM ET, will be webcast on the SEC's website.

data.title: Century Aluminum upgraded at Wolfe on higher aluminum price outlook
data.description:
Century Aluminum (NASDAQ:CENX) +4.9% in Tuesday's trading to two-and-a-half-year highs as Wolfe Research upgrades to Outperform from Peer Perform with a $27 price target, citing higher aluminum price forecasts and an increasingly likely sale of a 500 MW power connection at the company's currently idled Hawesville smelter.

Flowing through a higher alumina/aluminum price, Wolfe analyst Timna Tanners now forecasts Q4 EBITDA of $82M, up from a prior $73M, which estimated FY 2025 EBITDA rises to $342M from an earlier $265M projection.

Century (CENX) can benefit from higher alumina prices more than doubling in 2024, as its 2023 investment in Jamalco acts as a hedge of alumina input costs, Tanners says, and higher alumina costs should support higher aluminum prices, as China and Russia smelters recently shuttered in response to lofty costs.

While Tanners agrees with alumina skeptics that the recent $787/ton spot price does not look sustainable, the analyst says decades of weak alumina prices have discouraged refinery capacity, plus tougher bauxite mining conditions in Australia and Guinea should keep input costs higher for longer.

data.title: Amazon goes all in on AI with GitLab and PagerDuty
data.description:
Amazon Web Services (NASDAQ:AMZN) went all in on artificial intelligence during re:Invent in Las Vegas on Tuesday as it announced new AI partnerships with GitLab (NASDAQ:GTLB) and PagerDuty (NYSE:PD), revealed a set of new foundational models and a new video search tool with the startup Twelve Labs.

GitLab Duo and Amazon Q teamed up to offer AI-powered DevSecOps (development, security and operations) workflows with autonomous agents to create secure software at an accelerated rate. It is intended to help organizations streamline software development.

"By leveraging Amazon Q's advanced capabilities and integrating them into our unified data store, GitLab Duo with Amazon Q provides an enterprise-grade platform that accelerates and automates software development while ensuring security, compliance, and privacy," said Ashley Kramer, interim chief revenue officer, and chief marketing and strategy officer at GitLab.

AWS also revealed new generative AI and automation features across the PagerDuty Operations Cloud platform.

"PagerDuty and AWS are strengthening our 11-year partnership, which already supports nearly 6,000 joint customers, to further integrate generative AI into digital operations management," said PagerDuty CEO Jennifer Tejada.

AWS announced AI startup Twelve Labs is building and scaling its proprietary models on AWS. Twelve Labs has utilized AI to develop software that can search video similar to text.

"Nearly 80% of the world's data is in video, yet most of it is unsearchable," said Twelve Labs CEO Jae Lee. "We are now able to address this challenge, surfacing highly contextual videos to bring experiences to life, similar to how humans see, hear, and understand the world around us."

Finally, AWS revealed a new set of foundational AI models called Amazon Nova. These include Nova Micro, Nova Lite, Nova Pro, Nova Premier, Nova Canvas and Nova Reel. The capabilities of these models range from text-to-text only to the ability to convert text and images into videos.

"Inside Amazon, we have about 1,000 generative AI applications in motion, and we've had a bird's-eye view of what application builders are still grappling with," said Rohit Prasad, SVP of Amazon Artificial General Intelligence. "Our new Amazon Nova models are intended to help with these challenges for internal and external builders, and provide compelling intelligence and content generation while also delivering meaningful progress on latency, cost-effectiveness, customization, Retrieval Augmented Generation, and agentic capabilities."
